tools.name: unity_advanced_tool
tools.description: Execute an advanced/specialized Unity tool by name. Use unity_list_advanced_tools to discover available tools and their parameters. This provides access to 200+ specialized tools for animation, prefabs, physics, shaders, terrain, particles, UI, profiling, and more.

params.description: Parameters to pass to the tool. Use unity_list_advanced_tools to see required parameters.

tools.name: unity_agent_log
tools.description: Get the action log for a specific agent, showing the last 100 actions with timestamps.

agentId.description: The agent ID to get logs for

tools.name: unity_agents_list
tools.description: List all active agent sessions connected to the AB Unity MCP bridge. Shows each agent's ID, connection time, last activity, current action, total actions count, queued/completed request counts, and average response time.

tools.name: unity_amplify_add_node
tools.description: Add a node to the currently open Amplify Shader Editor graph. The ASE window must be open with a shader loaded. Use unity_amplify_get_node_types to discover available node types first.

nodeType.description: Full type name of the node (e.g., 'AmplifyShaderEditor.ColorNode', 'AmplifyShaderEditor.SimpleMultiplyOpNode', 'AmplifyShaderEditor.SamplerNode')

tools.name: unity_amplify_close
tools.description: Close the Amplify Shader Editor window. By default saves the graph before closing to prevent save dialogs.

save.description: Save the graph before closing (default: true)

tools.name: unity_amplify_connect
tools.description: Connect two nodes in the Amplify Shader Editor graph. Connects an output port of one node to an input port of another node.

outputNodeId.description: ID of the source node (output side)

outputPortId.description: Port index on the output node (0-based)

inputNodeId.description: ID of the destination node (input side)

inputPortId.description: Port index on the input node (0-based)

tools.name: unity_amplify_create_from_template
tools.description: Create a new Amplify shader from a predefined template (surface, unlit, urp_lit, transparent, post_process). The shader file is created and can then be opened in ASE.

path.description: Asset path for the new shader (e.g., 'Assets/Shaders/MyShader.shader')

template.description: Template type: 'surface' (Standard PBR), 'unlit', 'urp' or 'urp_lit' (URP Lit), 'transparent', 'post_process' or 'postprocess'

tools.name: unity_amplify_create_shader
tools.description: Create a new Amplify Shader Editor shader file with proper ASE serialization markers. The shader can then be opened in ASE for visual editing.

path.description: Asset path for the new shader (e.g., 'Assets/Shaders/MyShader.shader')

shaderName.description: Shader name in the shader dropdown (e.g., 'Custom/MyShader')

tools.name: unity_amplify_disconnect
tools.description: Disconnect a specific port on a node in the Amplify Shader Editor graph.

isInput.description: True to disconnect an input port, false for output port (default: true)

tools.name: unity_amplify_disconnect_all
tools.description: Remove all connections from a specific node in the Amplify Shader Editor graph (both input and output connections).

nodeId.description: Unique ID of the node to disconnect

tools.name: unity_amplify_duplicate_node
tools.description: Duplicate a node in the Amplify Shader Editor graph. Creates a new node of the same type at a slight offset from the original.

nodeId.description: Unique ID of the node to duplicate

tools.name: unity_amplify_focus_node
tools.description: Focus the Amplify Shader Editor view on a specific node, centering and optionally zooming to it.

nodeId.description: Unique ID of the node to focus on

tools.name: unity_amplify_get_connections
tools.description: Get all connections between nodes in the currently open Amplify Shader Editor graph. Shows which output ports connect to which input ports.

tools.name: unity_amplify_get_node_types
tools.description: List all available Amplify Shader Editor node types by reflecting over the ASE assembly. Returns type names, categories, and descriptions. Requires Amplify to be installed.

tools.name: unity_amplify_get_nodes
tools.description: Get all nodes in the currently open Amplify Shader Editor graph. Returns node IDs, types, positions, and port counts. The ASE window must be open with a shader loaded.

tools.name: unity_amplify_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about an Amplify shader: properties, render queue, pass count, and Amplify metadata (node count, version, features like custom expressions, functions, texture samples).

tools.name: unity_amplify_list
tools.description: List all shaders created with Amplify Shader Editor. Detects Amplify shaders by scanning for ASE serialization markers in .shader files. Only available when Amplify is installed.

filter.description: Filter by shader name or path

tools.name: unity_amplify_list_functions
tools.description: List all Amplify Shader Functions in the project. Functions are reusable node groups (similar to Shader Graph Sub Graphs). Only available when Amplify is installed.

tools.name: unity_amplify_master_node_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about the master/output node of the currently open Amplify shader graph, including all its input ports and properties.

tools.name: unity_amplify_move_node
tools.description: Move a node to a new position in the Amplify Shader Editor graph.

tools.name: unity_amplify_node_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific node in the Amplify Shader Editor graph, including all input/output ports with names, data types, and connection status.

tools.name: unity_amplify_open
tools.description: Open a shader in the Amplify Shader Editor window for visual editing. Only available when Amplify is installed.

tools.name: unity_amplify_remove_node
tools.description: Remove a node from the currently open Amplify Shader Editor graph by its unique ID. Cannot remove the master/output node.

nodeId.description: Unique ID of the node to remove (from unity_amplify_get_nodes)

tools.name: unity_amplify_save
tools.description: Save the currently open Amplify Shader Editor graph to disk. If the shader has never been saved, auto-determines a save path from the shader name or uses the provided path.

path.description: Optional asset path to save to (e.g. 'Assets/Shaders/MyShader.shader'). Only needed if the shader has never been saved before.

tools.name: unity_amplify_set_node_property
tools.description: Set a property or field value on a node in the Amplify Shader Editor graph via reflection. If the property name is wrong, returns a list of available properties.

propertyName.description: Name of the property or field to set (e.g., 'm_defaultValue', 'PropertyName')

value.description: Value to set (will be parsed based on property type)

tools.name: unity_amplify_status
tools.description: Check if Amplify Shader Editor is installed in the project. Returns available commands, shader count, and function count. Only works when Amplify Shader Editor is imported.

tools.name: unity_animation_add_event
tools.description: Add an animation event that calls a function at a specific time during playback. Can pass string, int, or float parameters.

functionName.description: Name of the function to call on the animated GameObject

time.description: Time in seconds when the event fires

tools.name: unity_animation_add_keyframe
tools.description: Add a keyframe to an animation curve with full tangent and weight control. Creates the curve if it doesn't exist yet. Supports all weighted tangent modes.

relativePath.description: Relative path of the animated object (empty for root)

time.description: Time in seconds for the keyframe

weightedMode.description: Weighted mode: None, In, Out, Both (optional)

tools.name: unity_animation_add_layer
tools.description: Add a new layer to an Animator Controller.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

tools.name: unity_animation_add_parameter
tools.description: Add a parameter to an Animator Controller (Float, Int, Bool, or Trigger).

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to add

defaultValue.description: Default value for the parameter (not applicable to Trigger)

tools.name: unity_animation_add_state
tools.description: Add a state to an Animator Controller layer. Can optionally assign an animation clip and set as default state.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

clipPath.description: Optional asset path of an AnimationClip to assign

isDefault.description: Set as the default state for this layer

tools.name: unity_animation_add_transition
tools.description: Add a transition between states in an Animator Controller. Supports conditions, exit time, and AnyState transitions.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

sourceState.description: Name of the source state (not needed if fromAnyState is true)

fromAnyState.description: Create transition from Any State (default: false)

hasExitTime.description: Whether the transition uses exit time

hasFixedDuration.description: Whether duration is in fixed time

tools.name: unity_animation_assign_controller
tools.description: Assign an Animator Controller to a GameObject (adds Animator component if needed).

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the target GameObject

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller to assign

tools.name: unity_animation_clip_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about an Animation Clip: curves, length, events, loop settings.

path.description: Asset path of the Animation Clip

tools.name: unity_animation_controller_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about an Animator Controller: layers, states, transitions, parameters.

path.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

tools.name: unity_animation_create_blend_tree
tools.description: Create a blend tree in an Animator Controller for smooth blending between animations. Supports 1D, 2D (FreeformDirectional, FreeformCartesian, SimpleDirectional), and Direct blend types.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

blendTreeName.description: Name for the blend tree state

blendType.description: Blend type: Simple1D, FreeformDirectional2D, FreeformCartesian2D, SimpleDirectional2D, Direct (default Simple1D)

motions.description: Array of motion entries with clipPath, threshold (1D), position (2D {x,y}), timeScale

tools.name: unity_animation_create_clip
tools.description: Create a new empty Animation Clip asset.

loop.description: Whether the clip should loop (default: false)

tools.name: unity_animation_create_controller
tools.description: Create a new Animator Controller asset at the specified path.

tools.name: unity_animation_get_blend_tree
tools.description: Get the structure and configuration of a blend tree including all child motions, thresholds, positions, and time scales.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

stateName.description: Name of the blend tree state

tools.name: unity_animation_get_curve_keyframes
tools.description: Get all keyframes from an animation curve binding with full tangent data (inTangent, outTangent, inWeight, outWeight, weightedMode). Essential for precise animation editing.

relativePath.description: Relative path of the animated object (empty for root)

tools.name: unity_animation_get_events
tools.description: List all animation events on a clip with their function names, times, and parameters.

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_curve
tools.description: Remove an entire animation curve binding from a clip. Useful for cleaning up or restructuring animations.

relativePath.description: Relative path of the animated object (empty for root)

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_event
tools.description: Remove an animation event by its index. Use get_animation_events first to find the index.

eventIndex.description: Zero-based index of the event to remove

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_keyframe
tools.description: Remove a keyframe from an animation curve by its index. Use get_curve_keyframes first to find the index.

relativePath.description: Relative path of the animated object

keyframeIndex.description: Zero-based index of the keyframe to remove

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_layer
tools.description: Remove a layer from an Animator Controller by index. Cannot remove the base layer (index 0).

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

layerIndex.description: Index of the layer to remove (must be > 0)

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_parameter
tools.description: Remove a parameter from an Animator Controller by name.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to remove

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_state
tools.description: Remove a state from an Animator Controller layer.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

stateName.description: Name of the state to remove

tools.name: unity_animation_remove_transition
tools.description: Remove a transition from an Animator Controller state. Can target by source/destination names or by index.

controllerPath.description: Asset path of the Animator Controller

sourceState.description: Name of the source state (use 'AnyState' for any-state transitions)

destinationState.description: Name of the destination state (helps identify which transition)

transitionIndex.description: Index of the transition on the source state (alternative to destinationState)

tools.name: unity_animation_set_clip_curve
tools.description: Set an animation curve on a clip. Define keyframes to animate any property (position, rotation, scale, custom).

clipPath.description: Asset path of the Animation Clip

relativePath.description: Relative path to the animated object (empty for root)

keyframes.description: Array of keyframes with time and value

tools.name: unity_animation_set_clip_settings
tools.description: Set animation clip settings: looping, root motion, mirroring, speed, frame rate, and more.

tools.name: unity_asmdef_add_references
tools.description: Add assembly references to an existing .asmdef file. Supports assembly names (e.g. 'Unity.TextMeshPro') which are auto-resolved to GUID format, or direct GUID refs.

path.description: Asset path of the .asmdef file to modify

references.description: Assembly names or GUID references to add (e.g. ['Unity.TextMeshPro', 'MyGame.Core'])

tools.name: unity_asmdef_create
tools.description: Create a new Assembly Definition (.asmdef) file for code containerisation and compilation optimisation. Assembly definitions split your project code into separate assemblies, reducing recompilation time and enforcing clean dependency boundaries.

rootNamespace.description: Root namespace for scripts in this assembly (e.g. 'MyGame.Runtime')

references.description: Assembly references â€” names (e.g. 'Unity.TextMeshPro') or GUID refs (e.g. 'GUID:xxx')

includePlatforms.description: Only compile for these platforms. Common: 'Editor', 'Android', 'iOS', 'StandaloneWindows64', 'StandaloneOSX', 'StandaloneLinux64', 'WebGL'

excludePlatforms.description: Compile for all platforms EXCEPT these

allowUnsafeCode.description: Allow unsafe C# code blocks (default: false)

autoReferenced.description: Automatically referenced by predefined assemblies (default: true)

noEngineReferences.description: Don't reference UnityEngine (for pure C# libraries, default: false)

precompiledReferences.description: Precompiled DLL references (when overrideReferences is true)

defineConstraints.description: Define constraints â€” assembly only compiles when ALL symbols are defined (e.g. 'UNITY_EDITOR', 'ENABLE_INPUT_SYSTEM')

tools.name: unity_asmdef_create_ref
tools.description: Create an Assembly Definition Reference (.asmref) file. This lets you include scripts from a different folder into an existing assembly, useful for extending packages or splitting code across directories while keeping them in the same compilation unit.

reference.description: Name of the target assembly definition to reference (e.g. 'MyGame.Runtime')

tools.name: unity_asmdef_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about an Assembly Definition file: name, references, platforms, settings.

tools.name: unity_asmdef_list
tools.description: List all Assembly Definition files in the project. Returns name, path, reference count, and platform info for each.

includePackages.description: Also list assembly definitions from Packages/ (default: false)

tools.name: unity_asmdef_remove_references
tools.description: Remove assembly references from an existing .asmdef file.

path.description: Asset path of the .asmdef file to modify

references.description: Assembly names or GUID references to remove

tools.name: unity_asmdef_set_platforms
tools.description: Set the include/exclude platform lists for an assembly definition. Use includePlatforms to restrict to specific platforms (e.g. ['Editor'] for editor-only code) or excludePlatforms to exclude certain platforms.

includePlatforms.description: Only compile for these platforms (e.g. ['Editor']). Clears excludePlatforms if set.

excludePlatforms.description: Compile for all platforms except these

tools.name: unity_asmdef_update_settings
tools.description: Update settings on an assembly definition: rootNamespace, allowUnsafeCode, autoReferenced, noEngineReferences, defineConstraints, etc. Only supply the properties you want to change.

tools.name: unity_asset_create_prefab
tools.description: Create a prefab from an existing GameObject in the scene.

gameObjectPath.description: Path of the source GameObject in the hierarchy

tools.name: unity_asset_delete
tools.description: Delete an asset from the project.

path.description: Asset path relative to project root (e.g. 'Assets/Scripts/MyScript.cs')

tools.name: unity_asset_import
tools.description: Import an external file into the Unity project as an asset.

sourcePath.description: Absolute path to the source file on disk

tools.name: unity_asset_instantiate_prefab
tools.description: Instantiate a prefab into the current scene.

tools.name: unity_asset_list
tools.description: List assets in the project. Can filter by path, type, and search term.

type.description: Asset type filter: Script, Scene, Prefab, Material, Texture, AudioClip, AnimationClip, Shader, Font, Mesh, Model

maxResults.description: Maximum assets to return (default: 500). Use lower values for large projects.

tools.name: unity_audio_create_source
tools.description: Create or configure an AudioSource on a GameObject. Can attach to existing object or create new one.

name.description: Name for new GameObject (if not attaching to existing)

path.description: Path of existing GameObject to attach AudioSource to

tools.name: unity_audio_info
tools.description: Get info about all AudioSources and AudioListeners in the scene.

tools.name: unity_audio_set_global
tools.description: Set global audio settings (master volume, pause).

tools.name: unity_build
tools.description: Start a build of the Unity project for a target platform.

scenes.description: Scene paths to include (default: scenes in build settings)

tools.name: unity_component_add
tools.description: Add a component to a GameObject. Supports built-in types (Rigidbody, BoxCollider, AudioSource, Light, Camera, etc.) and custom scripts.

gameObjectPath.description: Path or name of the target GameObject

tools.name: unity_component_batch_wire
tools.description: Wire multiple object references in a single call. Efficient for setting up many references at once (e.g. wiring a UI manager to all its panels, connecting enemy AI to patrol waypoints). Each entry specifies a target GameObject, property, and reference to assign.

references.description: Array of reference assignments to perform

referenceComponentType.description: Component type on the referenced GameObject

tools.name: unity_component_get_properties
tools.description: Get all serialized properties of a component on a GameObject.

gameObjectPath.description: Path or name of the target GameObject

tools.name: unity_component_get_referenceable
tools.description: Discover what objects can be assigned to an ObjectReference property. Returns matching scene objects and project assets filtered by the expected type. Useful before wiring references to know what's available.

tools.name: unity_component_remove
tools.description: Remove a component from a GameObject.

gameObjectPath.description: Path or name of the target GameObject

componentType.description: Type name of the component to remove

index.description: Index if multiple components of same type (default: 0)

tools.name: unity_component_set_property
tools.description: Set a property value on a component. Supports floats, ints, strings, bools, vectors, colors, and object references. For ObjectReference properties, pass value as: an asset path string, a scene object name string, null to clear, or an object with {assetPath}, {instanceId}, or {gameObject, componentType}.

gameObjectPath.description: Path or name of target GameObject

propertyName.description: Name of the property to set

value.description: Value to set (type depends on property). For ObjectReference: string asset path, string scene object name, null, or {assetPath?, instanceId?, gameObject?, componentType?}

tools.name: unity_component_set_reference
tools.description: Set an object reference on a component property. Dedicated tool for wiring references between GameObjects, components, and assets. More powerful than set_property for ObjectReference fields â€” supports resolution by asset path, scene GameObject name, component type, or instance ID.

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the target GameObject

instanceId.description: Instance ID of the target GameObject (alternative to path)

componentType.description: Component type containing the property (optional â€” will auto-search all components)

propertyName.description: Name of the ObjectReference property to set

referenceGameObject.description: Name or hierarchy path of a scene GameObject to assign

referenceComponentType.description: When referencing a scene object, get a specific component instead of the GameObject itself (e.g. 'Camera', 'AudioSource')

referenceInstanceId.description: Instance ID of the object to assign

clear.description: Set to true to clear/null the reference

tools.name: unity_console_clear
tools.description: Clear the Unity console log.

tools.name: unity_console_log
tools.description: Get recent Unity console log messages (errors, warnings, info). Useful for debugging.

count.description: Number of recent messages to retrieve (default: 50)

tools.name: unity_constraint_add
tools.description: Add an animation constraint (position, rotation, scale, aim, parent, lookat) to a GameObject with optional source.

type.description: Constraint type: position, rotation, scale, aim, parent, lookat

tools.name: unity_constraint_info
tools.description: Get all constraints on a GameObject with their type, active state, weight, and source count.

tools.name: unity_debugger_enable
tools.description: Enable or disable the Frame Debugger. When enabled, Unity pauses rendering after a specific draw call so you can inspect the GPU state. Uses reflection to access internal Unity APIs (Unity 6+).

enabled.description: true to enable, false to disable the Frame Debugger

tools.name: unity_debugger_event_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific Frame Debugger event: shader name, pass, keywords, vertex/index/instance counts, render target, batch break cause, and mesh info. The Frame Debugger must be enabled first.

eventIndex.description: The event index to inspect (from unity_debugger_events list)

tools.name: unity_debugger_events
tools.description: List all rendering events (draw calls) captured by the Frame Debugger. The Frame Debugger must be enabled first. Returns event index, type, and name for each draw call.

tools.name: unity_editor_ping
tools.description: Check if the Unity Editor bridge is running and responsive. Returns editor version, project name, and connection status.

tools.name: unity_editor_state
tools.description: Get the current Unity Editor state: play mode, compilation status, active scene, project path.

tools.name: unity_editorprefs_delete
tools.description: Delete an EditorPrefs key.

tools.name: unity_editorprefs_get
tools.description: Get an EditorPrefs value by key. Specify type: string (default), int, float, bool.

type.description: Value type: string, int, float, bool

tools.name: unity_editorprefs_set
tools.description: Set an EditorPrefs value. Specify type: string (default), int, float, bool.

type.description: Value type: string, int, float, bool

tools.name: unity_execute_code
tools.description: Execute arbitrary C# code inside the Unity Editor. The code runs in the editor context with access to all Unity APIs. Useful for one-off operations, queries, and automation. Return values are serialized to JSON.

code.description: C# code to execute. Must be a valid method body. Access UnityEngine and UnityEditor namespaces. Use 'return' to send data back.

tools.name: unity_execute_menu_item
tools.description: Execute a Unity Editor menu command by its path (e.g. 'File/Save', 'GameObject/3D Object/Cube', 'Window/General/Console').

tools.name: unity_gameobject_create
tools.description: Create a new GameObject in the scene. Can specify primitive type (Cube, Sphere, Capsule, Cylinder, Plane, Quad), parent, and initial transform.

primitiveType.description: Optional primitive: Cube, Sphere, Capsule, Cylinder, Plane, Quad, Empty

parent.description: Path or name of parent GameObject (optional)

tools.name: unity_gameobject_delete
tools.description: Delete a GameObject from the scene by path or name.

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the GameObject to delete

tools.name: unity_gameobject_duplicate
tools.description: Duplicate a GameObject with all its children and components.

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the GameObject to duplicate

newName.description: Name for the duplicate (default: original name + ' (Copy)')

tools.name: unity_gameobject_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about a specific GameObject: transform, components, children, active state, tags, layer.

tools.name: unity_gameobject_reparent
tools.description: Move a GameObject under a new parent in the hierarchy.

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the GameObject to move

newParent.description: Path of the new parent (empty string for scene root)

worldPositionStays.description: Maintain world position after reparenting (default: true)

tools.name: unity_gameobject_set_active
tools.description: Set a GameObject active or inactive.

path.description: Hierarchy path or name of the GameObject

active.description: Whether the GameObject should be active

tools.name: unity_gameobject_set_transform
tools.description: Set the transform (position, rotation, scale) of a GameObject.

local.description: If true, set local transform instead of world (default: false)

tools.name: unity_get_compilation_errors
tools.description: Get C# compilation errors and warnings from the Unity Editor. Uses CompilationPipeline directly — independent of the console log buffer. Not affected by console clear or Play Mode log flooding. Returns errors from the last compilation cycle. Use this instead of unity_console_log when diagnosing script compilation issues.

count.description: Max number of entries to retrieve (default: 50)

tools.name: unity_get_project_context
tools.description: Get project-specific context and documentation that the team has prepared for AI agents. This includes project guidelines, architecture docs, game design documents, networking rules, and any other project knowledge stored in Assets/MCP/Context/. Call this without arguments to get ALL context, or specify a category for a specific document. IMPORTANT: Call this early in your session to understand the project's conventions and architecture.

category.description: Optional: specific context category to fetch (e.g. 'ProjectGuidelines', 'Architecture', 'GameDesign', 'NetworkingGuidelines', 'NetworkingCSP', or any custom category). Omit to get all available context.

tools.name: unity_graphics_asset_preview
tools.description: Get a visual preview thumbnail of any Unity asset (prefab, material, texture, mesh, etc.) as an inline image. Returns base64 PNG image that Claude can see directly.

tools.name: unity_graphics_game_capture
tools.description: Capture the Game View camera as an inline image. Returns base64 PNG that Claude can see directly. Use to see what the player sees.

cameraName.description: Name of camera to use (default: Camera.main / MainCamera tag)

tools.name: unity_graphics_lighting_summary
tools.description: Get a summary of all lights in the scene, or details about a specific light. Returns type, color, intensity, range, spot angle, shadow settings, and render mode.

lightName.description: Optional: name of a specific light to get details for. If omitted, returns all lights.

tools.name: unity_graphics_material_info
tools.description: Get detailed material information: shader name, render queue, all properties (colors, floats, vectors, textures), keywords, and a visual preview thumbnail. Works on material assets or scene GameObjects.

assetPath.description: Material asset path (e.g. 'Assets/Materials/Wood.mat'). Use this OR objectPath.

objectPath.description: Scene GameObject path to get material from its Renderer. Use this OR assetPath.

materialIndex.description: Material index on the Renderer (default: 0). Only used with objectPath.

includePreview.description: Include a base64 PNG preview thumbnail (default: true)

tools.name: unity_graphics_mesh_info
tools.description: Get detailed mesh geometry information: vertex count, triangle count, submeshes, UV channels, blend shapes, bone count, bounds. Works on mesh assets or scene GameObjects with MeshFilter/SkinnedMeshRenderer.

assetPath.description: Mesh asset path (e.g. 'Assets/Models/Character.fbx'). Use this OR objectPath.

objectPath.description: Scene GameObject path to get mesh from its MeshFilter or SkinnedMeshRenderer. Use this OR assetPath.

tools.name: unity_graphics_prefab_render
tools.description: Render a prefab from a configurable angle as an inline image. Returns base64 PNG that Claude can see directly. Great for previewing 3D models and prefabs.

rotationY.description: Horizontal rotation angle in degrees (default: 30). Controls left-right viewing angle.

rotationX.description: Vertical rotation angle in degrees (default: 20). Controls up-down viewing angle.

padding.description: Padding multiplier around the object (default: 1.2). Higher = more space around object.

tools.name: unity_graphics_renderer_info
tools.description: Get detailed Renderer component information: renderer type, materials list, mesh reference, bounds, shadow settings, sorting layer, lightmap index. Use for rendering analysis.

objectPath.description: Scene GameObject path with a Renderer component

tools.name: unity_graphics_scene_capture
tools.description: Capture the current Scene View as an inline image. Returns base64 PNG that Claude can see directly. Use to visually inspect the scene layout.

tools.name: unity_graphics_texture_info
tools.description: Get detailed texture information: dimensions, format, compression, mipmaps, memory estimate, import settings, and a visual preview thumbnail. Use for texture analysis and optimization.

previewSize.description: Preview thumbnail size in pixels (default: 128). Set 0 to skip preview.

tools.name: unity_hub_available_releases
tools.description: List Unity Editor versions available for download from Unity Hub.

tools.name: unity_hub_get_install_path
tools.description: Get the current default installation path for Unity Editors.

tools.name: unity_hub_install_editor
tools.description: Install a specific Unity Editor version. Optionally include platform modules (android, ios, webgl, linux, macos, windows-il2cpp).

modules.description: Optional modules to install: android, android-sdk-ndk-tools, android-open-jdk, ios, webgl, linux-il2cpp, mac-il2cpp, windows-il2cpp, etc.

tools.name: unity_hub_install_modules
tools.description: Install additional platform modules to an already-installed Unity Editor version.

modules.description: Modules to add: android, ios, webgl, etc.

tools.name: unity_hub_list_editors
tools.description: List all Unity Editor versions currently installed via Unity Hub, including their installation paths.

tools.name: unity_hub_set_install_path
tools.description: Set the default installation directory for Unity Editors.

tools.name: unity_input_add_action
tools.description: Add an action to an action map in an Input Action Asset.

tools.name: unity_input_add_binding
tools.description: Add a simple (non-composite) binding to an action. Use for single-key bindings like '<Keyboard>/space' or '<Gamepad>/buttonSouth'.

actionName.description: Name of the action to bind to

tools.name: unity_input_add_composite_binding
tools.description: Add a composite binding (e.g. WASD, arrows) to an action. Composites combine multiple keys into a single value (1DAxis for up/down, 2DVector for WASD).

actionName.description: Name of the action to bind to

compositeType.description: Composite type path: '1DAxis' for pos/neg axis, '2DVector' for 4-directional (default: '1DAxis')

parts.description: Composite parts â€” each has a 'name' (e.g. 'positive','negative','up','down','left','right') and 'path' (e.g. '<Keyboard>/w')

path.description: Input binding path for this part (e.g. '<Keyboard>/w')

tools.name: unity_input_add_map
tools.description: Add a new action map to an Input Action Asset.

mapName.description: Name of the action map to add (e.g. 'Gameplay', 'UI')

tools.name: unity_input_create
tools.description: Create a new Input Action Asset (.inputactions file) for Unity's Input System. Supports optional initial action maps.

maps.description: Optional initial action maps to create

tools.name: unity_input_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about an Input Action Asset: maps, actions, bindings, and control schemes.

tools.name: unity_input_remove_action
tools.description: Remove an action (and its bindings) from an action map.

actionName.description: Name of the action to remove

tools.name: unity_input_remove_map
tools.description: Remove an action map from an Input Action Asset.

mapName.description: Name of the action map to remove

tools.name: unity_lighting_create
tools.description: Create a new light in the scene (Point, Directional, Spot, or Area).

tools.name: unity_lighting_create_light_probe_group
tools.description: Create a light probe group in the scene.

tools.name: unity_lighting_create_reflection_probe
tools.description: Create a reflection probe in the scene.

tools.name: unity_lighting_info
tools.description: Get info about all lights in the scene plus environment/fog settings.

tools.name: unity_lighting_set_environment
tools.description: Set environment lighting: ambient mode/color, fog, skybox material.

tools.name: unity_list_advanced_tools
tools.description: List all available advanced/specialized Unity tools organized by category. These tools are not directly exposed but can be called via unity_advanced_tool. Categories include: uma, animation, prefab, physics, lighting, audio, shadergraph, amplify, terrain, particle, navmesh, ui, texture, profiler, memory, settings, input, asmdef, scriptableobject, constraint, lod, editorprefs, playerprefs, vfx, graphics, sceneview, and more.

category.description: Filter by category name (e.g. "animation", "prefab", "shadergraph"). Omit for full list.

tools.name: unity_list_instances
tools.description: List all running Unity Editor instances that the MCP can connect to. Returns each instance's project name, port, Unity version, and whether it's a ParrelSync clone. Use this to see which Unity projects are currently open before selecting one to work with. IMPORTANT: When multiple instances are detected, always call this first and then use unity_select_instance to choose which project to target.

refresh.description: Force a fresh discovery scan (default: true). Set to false to use cached results.

tools.name: unity_lod_create
tools.description: Create or configure a LODGroup on a GameObject with specified number of LOD levels.

tools.name: unity_lod_info
tools.description: Get LODGroup info: LOD levels, screen transition heights, renderer counts.

tools.name: unity_material_create
tools.description: Create a new material asset with a specified shader and properties.

properties.description: Additional shader properties as key-value pairs

tools.name: unity_memory_breakdown
tools.description: Get detailed memory breakdown by asset type: textures, meshes, materials, shaders, audio clips, animation clips, fonts, render textures, and scriptable objects. Shows count, total size, and optionally top assets per category. Works without the Memory Profiler package (uses built-in Profiler APIs).

includeDetails.description: If true, include top assets per category with names, sizes, and asset paths (default: false)

maxPerCategory.description: Max assets to list per category when includeDetails=true (default: 5)

tools.name: unity_memory_snapshot
tools.description: Take a detailed memory snapshot using the Memory Profiler package (com.unity.memoryprofiler). Requires the package to be installed. The snapshot can be inspected in the Memory Profiler window. Returns an error with alternatives if the package is not installed.

path.description: Directory to save snapshot in (default: temp cache)

tools.name: unity_memory_status
tools.description: Check Memory Profiler status: whether the com.unity.memoryprofiler package is installed, available commands, and a quick memory summary. Always call this first before other memory profiler commands.

tools.name: unity_memory_top_assets
tools.description: Get the top N memory-consuming assets across all types. Shows asset name, type, size, and asset path. Optionally filter by type (texture, mesh, audio, material, shader, animation, font, rendertexture). Works without the Memory Profiler package.

count.description: Number of top assets to return (default: 20)

type.description: Filter by asset type: texture, rendertexture, mesh, audio, material, shader, animation, font (default: all)

tools.name: unity_mppm_activate_scenario
tools.description: Load/activate a specific scenario by its asset path.

tools.name: unity_mppm_info
tools.description: Get multiplayer play mode information including CurrentPlayer state, tags, and MPPM package version.

tools.name: unity_mppm_list_scenarios
tools.description: List all MPPM (Multiplayer PlayMode) scenarios in the project with details about instances and configurations.

tools.name: unity_mppm_start
tools.description: Start the currently active scenario.

tools.name: unity_mppm_status
tools.description: Get the current scenario status including running state, active scenario name, and progress.

tools.name: unity_mppm_stop
tools.description: Stop the running scenario.

tools.name: unity_navmesh_add_agent
tools.description: Add a NavMeshAgent component to a GameObject with optional settings (speed, angular speed, acceleration, stopping distance, radius, height).

tools.name: unity_navmesh_add_obstacle
tools.description: Add a NavMeshObstacle component to a GameObject.

carve.description: Whether the obstacle carves the NavMesh

tools.name: unity_navmesh_bake
tools.description: Bake the NavMesh for AI navigation. Optionally configure agent radius, height, slope, and climb.

agentClimb.description: Step height the agent can climb

tools.name: unity_navmesh_clear
tools.description: Clear all baked NavMeshes from the scene.

tools.name: unity_navmesh_info
tools.description: Get NavMesh information: vertex/triangle count, agents, obstacles, agent types.

tools.name: unity_navmesh_set_destination
tools.description: Set the destination for a NavMeshAgent (requires Play mode).

tools.name: unity_packages_add
tools.description: Add/install a Unity package by identifier (e.g. 'com.unity.cinemachine' or 'com.unity.cinemachine@3.0.0').

tools.name: unity_packages_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about an installed package including versions and dependencies.

tools.name: unity_packages_list
tools.description: List all installed Unity packages with their name, version, source, and status.

tools.name: unity_packages_remove
tools.description: Remove/uninstall a Unity package by name.

tools.name: unity_packages_search
tools.description: Search for Unity packages in the registry.

tools.name: unity_particle_create
tools.description: Create a new Particle System with optional initial settings.

tools.name: unity_particle_info
tools.description: Get detailed Particle System info: main module, emission, shape, and all module enable states.

tools.name: unity_particle_playback
tools.description: Control particle system playback: play, stop, pause, restart, or clear.

action.description: play, stop, pause, restart, or clear

tools.name: unity_particle_set_emission
tools.description: Configure particle emission: rate over time, rate over distance.

tools.name: unity_particle_set_main
tools.description: Configure the main module of a Particle System: duration, lifetime, speed, size, gravity, simulation space.

tools.name: unity_particle_set_shape
tools.description: Configure the emission shape: Sphere, Hemisphere, Cone, Box, Circle, Edge, etc.

shapeType.description: Shape type: Sphere, Hemisphere, Cone, Box, Circle, Edge, Rectangle, etc.

radiusThickness.description: 0 = emit from surface only, 1 = emit from entire volume

tools.name: unity_physics_collision_matrix
tools.description: Get the physics collision matrix showing which layers collide with each other.

tools.name: unity_physics_overlap_box
tools.description: Find all colliders within a box volume.

tools.name: unity_physics_overlap_sphere
tools.description: Find all colliders within a sphere. Useful for area-of-effect queries.

tools.name: unity_physics_raycast
tools.description: Cast a ray in the physics world and return hit information. Supports single or all-hits mode.

layerMask.description: Layer mask for filtering (default: all layers)

all.description: If true, return all hits instead of just the first

tools.name: unity_physics_set_collision_layer
tools.description: Set whether two physics layers should collide or ignore each other.

ignore.description: If true, layers will ignore each other (default: true)

tools.name: unity_physics_set_gravity
tools.description: Get or set the global physics gravity vector.

gravity.description: New gravity vector (omit to just read current)

tools.name: unity_play_mode
tools.description: Control Unity Editor play mode: enter play, pause, or stop.

tools.name: unity_playerprefs_delete
tools.description: Delete a PlayerPrefs key.

tools.name: unity_playerprefs_delete_all
tools.description: Delete ALL PlayerPrefs. Use with caution.

tools.name: unity_playerprefs_get
tools.description: Get a PlayerPrefs value by key. Specify type: string (default), int, float.

tools.name: unity_playerprefs_set
tools.description: Set a PlayerPrefs value. Specify type: string (default), int, float.

tools.name: unity_prefab_add_component
tools.description: Add a component to a GameObject inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Supports built-in types (Rigidbody, BoxCollider, etc.) and custom scripts.

prefabPath.description: Path within the prefab hierarchy. Empty = root.

tools.name: unity_prefab_add_gameobject
tools.description: Create a new child GameObject inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Optionally create as a primitive (Cube, Sphere, etc.) with position/rotation/scale.

prefabPath.description: Parent path within the prefab hierarchy. Empty = add under root.

tools.name: unity_prefab_apply_overrides
tools.description: Apply all overrides from a prefab instance in the scene back to the source prefab asset.

path.description: Hierarchy path of the prefab instance

tools.name: unity_prefab_apply_variant_override
tools.description: Apply overrides from a prefab variant back to its base prefab. Can apply all overrides or filter by componentType and/or gameObject path. This pushes the variant's changes into the base so all variants see them.

assetPath.description: Asset path of the prefab variant whose overrides to apply

componentType.description: Optional: only apply overrides for this component type (e.g. 'MeshRenderer')

gameObject.description: Optional: only apply overrides on this GameObject path within the prefab (e.g. 'Body/Head')

applyAll.description: If true, apply ALL overrides to the base. Default false.

tools.name: unity_prefab_compare_variant
tools.description: Compare a prefab variant to its base prefab. Returns all overrides: modified properties, added/removed components, and added/removed GameObjects. The variant must be a Prefab Variant asset.

assetPath.description: Asset path of the prefab variant to compare, e.g. 'Assets/Prefabs/PlayerVariant.prefab'

tools.name: unity_prefab_create_variant
tools.description: Create a prefab variant from an existing base prefab. Variants inherit from the base and can override specific properties.

basePrefabPath.description: Asset path of the base prefab

variantPath.description: Asset path for the new variant

tools.name: unity_prefab_get_hierarchy
tools.description: Get the full hierarchy tree of a prefab asset directly from disk â€” no scene instance needed. Shows all GameObjects, components, and nested children inside the prefab.

tools.name: unity_prefab_get_properties
tools.description: Read all serialized properties of a component on a GameObject inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Use prefabPath to target nested children (e.g. 'Body/Head').

prefabPath.description: Path within the prefab hierarchy to the target GameObject (e.g. 'Body/Head'). Empty or omitted = prefab root.

tools.name: unity_prefab_info
tools.description: Get detailed prefab information: overrides, variant status, added/removed components. Works on both prefab assets and scene instances.

path.description: Hierarchy path of a prefab instance in the scene

instanceId.description: Instance ID of a prefab instance

tools.name: unity_prefab_remove_component
tools.description: Remove a component from a GameObject inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed.

prefabPath.description: Path within the prefab hierarchy. Empty = root.

index.description: Index if multiple components of same type (default: 0)

tools.name: unity_prefab_remove_gameobject
tools.description: Delete a child GameObject from inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Cannot remove the prefab root.

prefabPath.description: Path to the child GameObject to remove (e.g. 'Body/OldPart'). Cannot be empty (can't delete root).

tools.name: unity_prefab_revert_overrides
tools.description: Revert all overrides on a prefab instance, restoring it to match the source prefab.

path.description: Hierarchy path of the prefab instance

tools.name: unity_prefab_revert_variant_override
tools.description: Revert overrides on a prefab variant so it matches its base prefab again. Can revert all overrides or filter by componentType and/or gameObject path.

assetPath.description: Asset path of the prefab variant to revert

componentType.description: Optional: only revert overrides for this component type

gameObject.description: Optional: only revert overrides on this GameObject path within the prefab

revertAll.description: If true, revert ALL overrides. Default false.

tools.name: unity_prefab_set_property
tools.description: Set a serialized property value on a component inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Supports floats, ints, strings, bools, vectors, colors, enums.

prefabPath.description: Path within the prefab hierarchy (e.g. 'Body/Head'). Empty = root.

propertyName.description: Name of the property to set

value.description: Value to set (type depends on property)

tools.name: unity_prefab_set_reference
tools.description: Wire an ObjectReference property on a component inside a prefab asset â€” no scene instance needed. Can reference project assets (materials, textures, prefabs, ScriptableObjects) or other GameObjects within the same prefab.

prefabPath.description: Path within the prefab hierarchy. Empty = root.

componentType.description: Component type (optional â€” searches all if omitted)

propertyName.description: Name of the ObjectReference property to set

referenceAssetPath.description: Asset path of the reference target (e.g. 'Assets/Materials/Red.mat')

referencePrefabPath.description: Path to another GameObject within the same prefab (e.g. 'Body/Head')

referenceComponentType.description: Get a specific component on the referenced prefab GameObject

clear.description: Set to true to null out the reference

tools.name: unity_prefab_transfer_variant_overrides
tools.description: Transfer (copy) overrides from one prefab variant to another variant of the same base. Reads the property modifications from the source variant and applies them to the target variant.

sourceAssetPath.description: Asset path of the source prefab variant to copy overrides from

targetAssetPath.description: Asset path of the target prefab variant to apply overrides to

tools.name: unity_prefab_unpack
tools.description: Unpack a prefab instance, converting it to regular GameObjects.

path.description: Hierarchy path of the prefab instance

completely.description: If true, unpack completely including nested prefabs (default: false)

tools.name: unity_prefab_variant_info
tools.description: Get variant information for a prefab asset: whether it's a variant, its base prefab path, and list all variants derived from a base prefab. Works on both base prefabs and variants.

assetPath.description: Asset path of the prefab (base or variant), e.g. 'Assets/Prefabs/Player.prefab'

tools.name: unity_profiler_analyze
tools.description: Run a comprehensive performance analysis combining memory, rendering stats, profiler frame data, and scene complexity. Returns optimization suggestions based on configurable thresholds (e.g. too many batches, high triangle count, excessive set-pass calls, GPU memory usage, shadow casters).

tools.name: unity_profiler_enable
tools.description: Enable or disable the Unity Profiler. Optionally enable deep profiling for detailed call stacks (has significant performance overhead).

enabled.description: true to start profiling, false to stop

deepProfile.description: Enable deep profiling for full call stacks (high overhead, default: false)

tools.name: unity_profiler_frame_data
tools.description: Get CPU profiler frame data as a hierarchical timing breakdown. Shows function names, total/self time, call counts, and GC allocations. The profiler must be enabled and have captured at least one frame.

frameIndex.description: Frame index to read (-1 for latest, default: -1)

minTimeMs.description: Minimum total time in ms to include an item (default: 0.1)

tools.name: unity_profiler_memory
tools.description: Get detailed memory usage breakdown: total allocated, reserved, Mono heap used/size, graphics driver memory, temp allocator size, and GC info. Values are returned in both bytes and human-readable MB.

tools.name: unity_profiler_stats
tools.description: Get current rendering statistics: draw calls, batches, triangles, vertices, set-pass calls, frame time, render time, shadow casters, and more. The profiler does NOT need to be enabled for this â€” stats come from UnityStats which is always available.

tools.name: unity_project_info
tools.description: Get project information: name, path, Unity version, render pipeline, packages, build settings.

tools.name: unity_queue_info
tools.description: Get the current state of the multi-agent request queue: total queued requests, active agents, per-agent queue depths, and completed cache size. Useful for monitoring when multiple agents are working on the same Unity project.

tools.name: unity_queue_ticket_status
tools.description: Check the status of a specific queue ticket by ID. Returns the ticket's current status (Queued, Executing, Completed, Failed, TimedOut), result data if completed, and timing information.

ticketId.description: The ticket ID returned from a queue submission

tools.name: unity_redo
tools.description: Redo the last undone operation in Unity Editor.

tools.name: unity_renderer_set_material
tools.description: Assign a material to a GameObject's renderer.

tools.name: unity_scene_hierarchy
tools.description: Get the full hierarchy tree of all GameObjects in the active scene, including their components and children.

maxNodes.description: Maximum total nodes to return (default: 5000). Use lower values for very large scenes to avoid timeouts.

parentPath.description: Only return hierarchy under this GameObject path (e.g. 'Canvas/Panel'). Useful for exploring specific subtrees in large scenes.

tools.name: unity_scene_info
tools.description: Get information about the currently open scene(s), including name, path, dirty state, and root game objects.

tools.name: unity_scene_new
tools.description: Create a new empty scene.

tools.name: unity_scene_open
tools.description: Open a scene by its asset path (relative to Assets/).

tools.name: unity_scene_save
tools.description: Save the current scene.

tools.name: unity_scene_stats
tools.description: Get comprehensive scene statistics: total objects, vertices, triangles, lights, cameras, colliders, and top component types.

tools.name: unity_sceneview_info
tools.description: Get Scene View camera info: pivot position, rotation, zoom, orthographic mode, 2D mode.

tools.name: unity_sceneview_set_camera
tools.description: Control the Scene View camera: set pivot, rotation, zoom, orthographic mode, look-at target, or frame selection.

pivot.description: Camera pivot position { x, y, z }

rotation.description: Camera rotation (Euler angles) { x, y, z }

lookAt.description: Point to look at { x, y, z }

tools.name: unity_screenshot_game
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the Game View. The screenshot is saved on the next frame render.

tools.name: unity_screenshot_scene
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the Scene View camera. Returns immediately with the saved file path.

tools.name: unity_script_create
tools.description: Create a new C# script file in the project with the given content.

className.description: Class name (defaults to filename without extension)

tools.name: unity_script_read
tools.description: Read the contents of a C# script file from the project.

tools.name: unity_script_update
tools.description: Update the contents of an existing C# script file.

content.description: New full C# source code content

tools.name: unity_scriptableobject_create
tools.description: Create a new ScriptableObject asset from a C# type. The type must already exist as a compiled script.

tools.name: unity_scriptableobject_info
tools.description: Get all serialized properties and values of a ScriptableObject asset.

tools.name: unity_scriptableobject_list_types
tools.description: List all available ScriptableObject types in the project. Useful for discovering what SO types can be created.

includeEngine.description: Include Unity engine types (default: false, project types only)

tools.name: unity_scriptableobject_set_field
tools.description: Set a field value on a ScriptableObject asset. Supports int, float, bool, string, and enum types.

value.description: Value to set (type depends on field)

tools.name: unity_search_assets
tools.description: Search for assets in the project by name, type, and folder. Uses Unity's AssetDatabase search.

tools.name: unity_search_by_component
tools.description: Find all GameObjects in the scene that have a specific component type. Returns their paths and instance IDs.

limit.description: Maximum results to return (default: 500). Use lower values on large scenes.

tools.name: unity_search_by_layer
tools.description: Find all GameObjects on a specific layer.

tools.name: unity_search_by_name
tools.description: Find all GameObjects whose name contains a pattern. Supports substring matching or regex.

regex.description: Use regex matching instead of substring (default: false)

tools.name: unity_search_by_shader
tools.description: Find all renderers using a specific shader.

shader.description: Shader name to search for (partial match)

tools.name: unity_search_by_tag
tools.description: Find all GameObjects with a specific tag.

tools.name: unity_search_missing_references
tools.description: Find all missing/broken object references and missing scripts in the scene. Essential for cleanup and debugging.

tools.name: unity_select_instance
tools.description: Select which Unity Editor instance to work with for this session. All subsequent unity_* commands will be routed to the selected instance. You must provide the port number of the instance (get it from unity_list_instances). IMPORTANT: Call unity_list_instances first to see available instances and their ports. PARALLEL SAFETY: After selecting, include 'port: <number>' as a parameter in ALL subsequent unity_* tool calls to guarantee routing to this instance even when multiple agents share the same MCP process.

port.description: The port number of the Unity instance to select (from unity_list_instances output).

tools.name: unity_selection_find_by_type
tools.description: Find all GameObjects in the scene that have a specific component type (e.g. 'Rigidbody', 'Camera', 'Light', 'AudioSource', or custom scripts).

tools.name: unity_selection_focus_scene_view
tools.description: Control the Scene View camera: frame a GameObject, set pivot/rotation/zoom, toggle orthographic.

path.description: GameObject to frame in scene view

instanceId.description: Instance ID of GameObject to frame

tools.name: unity_selection_get
tools.description: Get the currently selected GameObjects in the Unity Editor.

tools.name: unity_selection_set
tools.description: Set the editor selection to specific GameObjects.

instanceId.description: Instance ID of GameObject to select

tools.name: unity_set_object_reference
tools.description: [LEGACY â€” prefer unity_component_set_reference] Set an object reference property on a component via the prefab system. Use unity_component_set_reference instead for richer resolution options.

path.description: Hierarchy path of the target GameObject

componentType.description: Component type name (optional - will search all components)

propertyName.description: Name of the ObjectReference property to set

referencePath.description: Asset path of the reference (for assets like prefabs, materials, textures)

referenceGameObject.description: Name/path of a GameObject in the scene (for scene references)

tools.name: unity_settings_physics
tools.description: Get physics settings: gravity, solver iterations, sleep threshold, contact offset, bounce threshold.

tools.name: unity_settings_player
tools.description: Get player settings: company name, product name, version, color space, scripting backend, target architecture.

tools.name: unity_settings_quality
tools.description: Get current quality settings: level, shadows, anti-aliasing, LOD bias, vsync, and all quality levels available.

tools.name: unity_settings_render_pipeline
tools.description: Get information about the current render pipeline (Built-in, URP, HDRP).

tools.name: unity_settings_set_physics
tools.description: Modify physics settings like gravity, solver iterations, sleep threshold, etc.

tools.name: unity_settings_set_player
tools.description: Modify player settings like company name, product name, bundle version, run in background.

tools.name: unity_settings_set_quality_level
tools.description: Set the active quality level by name or index.

tools.name: unity_settings_set_time
tools.description: Modify time settings: fixed timestep, max delta time, time scale (for slow-mo or fast-forward).

timeScale.description: Time scale (1 = normal, 0.5 = half speed, 2 = double speed)

tools.name: unity_settings_time
tools.description: Get time settings: fixedDeltaTime, maximumDeltaTime, timeScale.

tools.name: unity_shader_get_properties
tools.description: Get exposed properties of any shader (.shader or .shadergraph). Shows property name, display name, type (Color, Vector, Float, Range, TexEnv), range limits, texture dimension, visibility.

shaderName.description: Shader name (e.g. 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'). Alternative to path.

tools.name: unity_shader_list
tools.description: List all shaders in the project (both .shader and .shadergraph files). Works without Shader Graph package. Filter by name, include/exclude built-in shaders.

filter.description: Filter shaders by name or path (case-insensitive)

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_add_node
tools.description: Add a new node to a Shader Graph. Supports common types: Add, Multiply, Subtract, Divide, Lerp, Color, Float, Vector2, Vector3, Vector4, Time, UV, Position, Normal, SampleTexture2D, Fresnel, Saturate, OneMinus, Power, Split, Combine. Also supports any type by full class name.

nodeType.description: Node type name (e.g., 'Add', 'Multiply', 'Color', 'SampleTexture2D') or full class name

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_connect
tools.description: Connect two nodes in a Shader Graph by creating an edge between an output slot and an input slot.

outputSlotId.description: Output slot ID on the source node

inputSlotId.description: Input slot ID on the target node

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_create
tools.description: Create a new Shader Graph from a template. Templates: urp_lit, urp_unlit, urp_sprite_lit, urp_sprite_unlit, urp_decal, hdrp_lit, hdrp_unlit, blank. Requires Shader Graph package.

path.description: Asset path for the new shader graph (e.g. 'Assets/Shaders/MyShader.shadergraph')

template.description: Template type: urp_lit, urp_unlit, urp_sprite_lit, urp_sprite_unlit, urp_decal, hdrp_lit, hdrp_unlit, blank (default: urp_lit)

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_disconnect
tools.description: Disconnect two nodes in a Shader Graph by removing the edge between them.

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_get_edges
tools.description: Get all edges (connections) in a Shader Graph. Returns source and target node IDs with slot IDs, showing how nodes are wired together.

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_get_node_types
tools.description: List all available Shader Graph node types by reflecting over the ShaderGraph assembly. Returns type names, categories, and full class names. Useful for discovering available nodes before adding them.

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_get_nodes
tools.description: Get all nodes in a Shader Graph file. Returns node IDs, types, positions, and basic property data by parsing the .shadergraph JSON. Essential for understanding graph structure before editing.

tools.name: unity_shadergraph_info

tools.name: unity_uma_create_overlay
tools.description:
Create a UMA OverlayDataAsset with the correct number of texture channels based on the UMA Material. Assigns textures to channels (diffuse, normal, etc.).

EXAMPLE CALL:
{
  "overlayName": "PT_Iron_Chest_Overlay",
  "outputFolder": "Assets/UMA/Overlays/Armor",
  "umaMaterialPath": "Assets/UMA/Content/UMA_Core/MaterialSamples/UMA_ClothesBase.asset",
  "textures": ["Assets/Textures/Chest_D.png", "Assets/Textures/Chest_N.png", "Assets/Textures/Chest_MR.png"]
}

CRITICAL: textures MUST be a flat JSON array of strings. Do NOT use dict format like {"0":"path"}. Parameter is outputFolder, NOT savePath.

umaMaterialPath.description: FULL asset path (NOT just the name) to the UMAMaterial. Must start with 'Assets/' and end with '.asset'. Example: 'Assets/UMA/Content/UMA_Core/MaterialSamples/UMA_ClothesBase.asset'. Must match the slot's material.

textures.description: Ordered array of texture asset paths, one per UMAMaterial channel index. Index 0 = diffuse/albedo, index 1 = normal map, index 2 = metallic/roughness. Use null or empty string for unused channels. Example: ["Assets/Textures/Chest_D.png", "Assets/Textures/Chest_N.png", null]. Do NOT use channel0/channel1/channel2 object format — must be a flat array of strings.

tools.name: unity_uma_create_race

raceName.description: Name for the new race (REQUIRED). Must be unique.

sourceRaceName.description: Name of an existing race to duplicate from (Global Library lookup). If provided, enters duplicate mode.

sourceRacePath.description: Direct asset path to the source RaceData. Alternative to sourceRaceName.

outputFolder.description: Folder to save the new RaceData (and base recipe). Defaults to same folder as source race in duplicate mode.

wardrobeSlots.description: Wardrobe slot list. In duplicate mode, overrides the source's list.

fixupRotations.description: Set to true for Blender FBX slots (default: copies source or true).

fbxPath.description: Asset path to a body FBX file (e.g. 'Assets/.../SK_Hu_F_FullBody.fbx'). Enables FBX mode: creates body slots, overlays, and base race recipe from the FBX.

umaMaterialPath.description: FULL asset path to the UMAMaterial for body slots (required in FBX mode). Must start with 'Assets/' and end with '.asset'.

duplicateBaseRecipe.description: In duplicate mode, also duplicate the base recipe with updated race name (default: true).

registerInLibrary.description: Register the new race in the UMA Global Library (default: true).

tools.name: unity_uma_create_slot
tools.description:
Create a UMA SlotDataAsset from an FBX SkinnedMeshRenderer. Automatically extracts weighted bones for keepList, fixes SlotName, initializes tags/Races arrays, and cleans up subfolder/parasite artifacts. Use unity_uma_inspect_fbx first to see available SMRs.

EXAMPLE CALL:
{
  "fbxPath": "Assets/Models/Armor/SK_Hu_M_IronChest.fbx",
  "smrName": "Chest",
  "slotName": "PT_Iron_Chest",
  "outputFolder": "Assets/UMA/Slots/Armor",
  "umaMaterialPath": "Assets/UMA/Content/UMA_Core/MaterialSamples/UMA_ClothesBase.asset"
}

smrName.description: Name of the SkinnedMeshRenderer inside the FBX (from inspect_fbx results)

outputFolder.description: Folder to save the slot asset (e.g. 'Assets/UMA/Slots/Armor')

umaMaterialPath.description: FULL asset path (NOT just the name) to the UMAMaterial. Must start with 'Assets/' and end with '.asset'. Example: 'Assets/UMA/Content/UMA_Core/MaterialSamples/UMA_ClothesBase.asset'

keepAllBones.description: If true, keep all bones instead of only weighted ones (default: false — auto-extracts keepList)

tools.name: unity_uma_create_wardrobe_from_fbx
tools.description:
Create all UMA assets from a single FBX file in one atomic call. Inspects the FBX for sub-meshes, creates SlotDataAssets (via UMA's CreateSlotData), creates OverlayDataAssets with texture deduplication, assembles WardrobeRecipes (recipeString v3), and registers everything in the Global Library. Supports multiple texture variants in a single call. This replaces the manual 5-step workflow (inspect + create-slot + create-overlay + create-recipe + register).

TEXTURE DETECTION: Textures are always read automatically from the Unity materials assigned to each FBX sub-mesh. No manual texture paths needed. Each variant reads from the FBX sharedMaterials (diffuse, normal, metallic/roughness).

EXAMPLE (single variant):
{ fbxPath: 'Assets/Models/Armor/SK_Hu_M_IronChest.fbx', outputFolder: 'Assets/UMA/Generated/IronChest', wardrobeSlot: 'Chest', umaMaterialPath: 'Assets/UMA/.../UMA_ClothesBase.asset', variants: [{ suffix: 'Iron' }] }

EXAMPLE (two variants from same FBX):
{ ..., variants: [{ suffix: 'Peasant' }, { suffix: 'Noble' }] }

CRITICAL RULES:
- variants[].suffix is REQUIRED (not name or variantName)
- Textures are always auto-read from FBX materials - no texture paths needed
- umaMaterialPath MUST be a full path starting with Assets/ and ending with .asset
- For custom texture control, use the individual tools (create_slot, create_overlay, create_wardrobe_recipe) instead

fbxPath.description: Asset path to the FBX file (e.g. 'Assets/Models/Armor/SK_Hu_M_IronChest.fbx')

outputFolder.description: Root folder for generated assets (e.g. 'Assets/UMA/GeneratedAssets/IronChest'). Sub-folders Slots/, Overlays/, Recipes/ are created automatically.

umaMaterialPath.description: FULL asset path (NOT just the name) to the UMAMaterial to assign to all slots. Must start with 'Assets/' and end with '.asset'. Example: 'Assets/UMA/Content/UMA_Core/MaterialSamples/UMA_ClothesBase.asset'

variants.description: Array of variant definitions. Each variant produces one WardrobeRecipe. Textures are auto-read from FBX materials for each sub-mesh. If two sub-meshes share identical textures they share the same overlay (deduplication).

suffix.description: Suffix used in asset naming for this variant (e.g. 'Iron', 'Gold', 'Peasant'). Appended to slot/overlay/recipe names.

recipeName.description: Optional base name for recipes. Defaults to FBX filename with prefix stripped (e.g. SK_Hu_M_ removed). Each variant appends _{suffix}_Recipe.

race.description: Race name for compatible races (default: auto-detect from FBX prefix, e.g. SK_Hu_M_ = HumanMaleDCS)

registerInGlobalLibrary.description: Whether to register all created assets in the UMA Global Library (default: true)

verifyAfterCreation.description: Whether to run VerifyRecipe on each created recipe after assembly (default: true)

tools.name: unity_uma_create_wardrobe_recipe

compatibleRaces.description: Array of race names this recipe is compatible with (e.g. ['HumanMaleDCS', 'HumanFemaleDCS'])

slots.description: Array of slot objects. Each slot has a slotName (the SlotDataAsset name) and an overlays array. Each overlay has overlayName and optional channelCount (default 3). For backward compatibility, a single overlayName at slot level is also accepted but overlays array is preferred.

overlays.description: Array of overlays stacked on this slot. Order matters: first overlay is the base layer.

channelCount.description: Number of texture channels for this overlay (default: 3 = diffuse+normal+mask)

overlayName.description: DEPRECATED: use overlays array instead. Single overlay name for backward compat.

channelCount.description: DEPRECATED: use overlays[].channelCount instead. Channel count when using single overlayName.

hides.description: Optional array of slot names to hide when this recipe is equipped (e.g. ['MaleUnderwear'])

displayValue.description: Optional display name shown in character creator UI

tools.name: unity_uma_edit_race
tools.description:
Edit properties of an existing UMA RaceData asset. Supports renaming (with automatic cascade to all WardrobeRecipes, base recipe, and DCA in scene), modifying wardrobe slots, physics properties, tags, and cross-compatibility settings.

RENAME CASCADE: When newRaceName is provided, automatically updates:
- The base recipe (compatibleRaces + recipeString JSON)
- All UMATextRecipe/WardrobeRecipe whose compatibleRaces or recipeString references the old name
- All DynamicCharacterAvatars in the current scene (activeRace.name)
- The .asset filename on disk
- The UMA Global Library

EXAMPLE (rename):
{ "raceName": "HumanRace", "newRaceName": "HumanMaleRace" }

EXAMPLE (add wardrobe slot):
{ "raceName": "HumanMaleRace", "addWardrobeSlots": ["TorsoAdditional"] }

EXAMPLE (full edit):
{ "raceName": "HumanRace", "newRaceName": "HumanMaleRace", "raceHeight": 1.8, "tags": ["playable", "male"] }

raceName.description: Current race name to edit (looked up in Global Library). Provide this OR racePath.

racePath.description: Direct asset path to the RaceData (e.g. 'Assets/.../HumanRace.asset'). Alternative to raceName.

newRaceName.description: New name for the race. Triggers cascade update on all recipes, DCA, and asset file.

wardrobeSlots.description: Complete replacement of wardrobe slot list. Mutually exclusive with addWardrobeSlots/removeWardrobeSlots.

addWardrobeSlots.description: Slots to add to the existing list (no duplicates).

removeWardrobeSlots.description: Slots to remove from the existing list.

fixupRotations.description: Set to true for Blender FBX slots.

tags.description: Replace all tags on the race.

backwardsCompatibleWith.description: Race names this race is cross-compatible with.

baseRaceRecipePath.description: Asset path to a new base race recipe (UMATextRecipe) to assign. Only if changing the base recipe reference.

updateRecipes.description: When renaming, update compatibleRaces + recipeString in all recipes (default: true).

updateDCA.description: When renaming, update DCA in current scene (default: true).

renameAssetFile.description: When renaming, also rename the .asset file on disk (default: true).

rebuildLibrary.description: Rebuild UMA Global Library after modifications (default: true).

tools.name: unity_uma_get_project_config
tools.description: Get project-specific UMA configuration by querying existing assets. Returns detected races, UMA materials, wardrobe slots, and recipe patterns. Call this before creating slots/overlays/recipes to auto-detect correct parameters.

tools.name: unity_uma_inspect_fbx
tools.description: Inspect an FBX file to list all SkinnedMeshRenderers with vertex counts, weighted bones (keepList), and bone counts. Essential first step before creating UMA slots — shows which SMRs are available and their bone data.

fbxPath.description: Asset path to the FBX file (e.g. 'Assets/Models/Armor.fbx')

tools.name: unity_uma_list_global_library
tools.description: List assets registered in the UMA Global Library. Can filter by type (Slot, Overlay, Race, WardrobeRecipe) and/or name pattern.

type.description: Filter by asset type. Accepts short names: 'Slot', 'Overlay', 'Race', 'WardrobeRecipe', full C# names: 'SlotDataAsset', 'OverlayDataAsset', 'RaceData', 'UMAWardrobeRecipe', or 'All' (default). Case-insensitive.

nameFilter.description: Optional substring filter on asset names (case-insensitive)

tools.name: unity_uma_list_uma_materials
tools.description: List all UMAMaterial assets in the project with their channel counts. Useful for choosing the right material when creating slots and overlays.

tools.name: unity_uma_list_wardrobe_slots
tools.description: List all wardrobe slot names available for a given UMA race (e.g. 'Chest', 'Legs', 'Feet', 'Helmet').

tools.name: unity_uma_rebuild_global_library
tools.description:
Rebuild or repair the UMA Global Library (UMAAssetIndexer). Use this when assets are missing from the library, after bulk creation/deletion, or when the library is in a corrupted state.

MODES:
- 'rebuild' (default): Full rebuild from project. Clears the library, rescans all UMA assets, and re-registers them. Equivalent to the 'Rebuild From Project' button in the UMA Global Library inspector.
- 'rebuild_with_text': Same as rebuild but also includes UMATextRecipe assets.
- 'repair': Light repair. Rebuilds type tables and removes broken/invalid entries without a full rescan. Faster but less thorough.

Returns asset counts before and after the operation with a breakdown by type (slots, overlays, recipes, races).

mode.description: Rebuild mode: 'rebuild' (full rescan, default), 'rebuild_with_text' (full + text recipes), or 'repair' (light cleanup).

tools.name: unity_uma_register_assets
tools.description: Register UMA assets (Slot, Overlay, or WardrobeRecipe) in the UMA Global Library so they are available at runtime. Must be called after creating assets.

assetPaths.description: Array of asset paths to register. Optional if folderPath is provided.

folderPath.description: Folder to scan recursively for all UMA assets (Slots, Overlays, Recipes). Alternative to listing each path manually.

tools.name: unity_uma_verify_recipe
tools.description: Validate a UMA WardrobeRecipe by checking that all referenced slots and overlays exist in the Global Library, that materialName is set on slots, and that fColors has enough entries for the colorIdx values used. Returns a list of issues (critical/warning) and a valid/invalid verdict. Essential post-creation check.

recipePath.description: Asset path to the WardrobeRecipe to verify (e.g. 'Assets/UMA/Recipes/MyRecipe.asset')

tools.name: unity_uma_wardrobe_equip
tools.description:
Equip or unequip a UMA wardrobe recipe on a DynamicCharacterAvatar in the scene. Automatically detects Play/Edit mode and uses the correct API path:
- Play Mode: Uses runtime DCA API (SetSlot/ClearSlot + BuildCharacter)
- Edit Mode: Modifies serialized preloadWardrobeRecipes list + triggers visual rebuild via GenerateSingleUMA

To UNEQUIP a slot, pass recipeName as null or omit it.

EXAMPLE (equip):
{ "gameObjectPath": "PlayerPrefabv2_TestWardrobe", "wardrobeSlot": "Helmet", "recipeName": "Helm_Peasant_Bl_Recipe" }

EXAMPLE (unequip):
{ "gameObjectPath": "PlayerPrefabv2_TestWardrobe", "wardrobeSlot": "Helmet", "recipeName": null }

WARDROBE SLOTS: Waist, Feet, Shoulders, Chest, Helmet, Legs, Hands, Hair, Complexion, Eyebrows, Beard, Eyes, Face, Ears, Underwear

gameObjectPath.description: Hierarchy path to the GameObject (or its parent) containing DynamicCharacterAvatar (e.g. 'PlayerPrefabv2_TestWardrobe')

recipeName.description: Recipe asset name (e.g. 'Helm_Peasant_Bl_Recipe'). null or omitted = unequip (clear the slot)
